expressions.english: to go to bed late
expressions.notes:
늦다 = to be late
늦게 is the adverb form of 늦다.

expressions.english: to go to bed early
to take a nap
to wake up late
Did you sleep well?
What are you still up doing?
expressions.notes:
잠 = sleep
안 = not
뭐 = what; something
해요 = to do

expressions.english: I'm sleepy. I want to sleep.
expressions.notes:
졸리다 = to be sleepy
-고 싶다 = to want to + V

expressions.english: What time did you go to bed yesterday?
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
몇 시 = what time

expressions.english: You must be tired, so go to bed early.
expressions.notes:
피곤하다 = to be tired, exhausted; to be tiring
-(으)ㄹ 테니 = (assuming that) something must + V

expressions.english: Good night and call me tomorrow.
expressions.notes:
내일 = tomorrow
연락하다 = to contact; to call, text

expressions.english: I couldn't sleep at all yesterday.
My sister is still sleeping.
expressions.notes:
동생 = younger sister/brother
아직 = (not) yet
-고 있다 = to be + V-ing

expressions.english: I'm going to sleep over at my friend's house tomorrow.
I got up late today, so I was late for school.
expressions.notes:
오늘 = today		학교 = school
늦잠을 자다 = to get up late, oversleep, sleep in
-에 지각하다 = to be late for + N

expressions.english: I had a strange dream while sleeping last night.
expressions.notes:
어젯밤 = last night
이상하다 = to be weird, odd, strange
꿈 = dream
꿈을 꾸다 = to have a dream; to dream

expressions.korean: 일요일에는 하루 종일 잠만 자고 싶어요.
expressions.english: On Sundays, I just want to sleep all day long.
expressions.notes:
일요일 = Sunday
하루 = one day
하루 종일 = all day long
-만 = only; just

expressions.korean: 너무 졸러서 점심 먹고 잠깐 낮잠을 잤어요.
expressions.english: I was so sleepy, so I took a short nap after having lunch.
expressions.notes:
너무 = too much, excessively; very
점심 = lunch
먹다 = to eat; to drink
잠깐 = short time; for a moment

expressions.korean: 어제 잠을 많이 못 자서 오늘 너무 피곤해요.
expressions.english: I couldn't sleep much yesterday, so I am very tired.
expressions.notes:
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.
못 = can't

expressions.korean: 요즘에 일이 많아서 잠을 많이 못 자고 있어요.
expressions.english: I have a lot of work to do these days, so I am not getting a lot of sleep.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion

expressions.korean: 어젯밤에 자고 있는데 친구에게서 전화가 왔어요.
expressions.english: Last night when I was sleeping, I got a phone call from a friend.
expressions.notes: 전화가 오다 = to get a phone call
